because.text: Recovered from an interrupted Kimi Code multi-agent run (session_ccff46ce, 2026-07-18): the audit found, but never wrote up or fixed, that verify_store is NOT TOTAL. After reporting a body schema errors it kept dereferencing the same fields it had just flagged as wrong-TYPED, so a type-confused record (prior:5, subject a string, actor a string plus a valid signature, ...) raised an uncaught TypeError/KeyError where Go returns a bounded report -- and because the exception escaped the whole-store loop, ONE malformed record denied verification of every other record (an availability vector). Also: settlement KeyError on a schema-invalid ancestor, and a why RecursionError on a deep linear prior-chain. Confirmed independently against HEAD 707b2c9 (10 crash shapes + availability + deep why). Fixed: type-guard the verify_store semantic block so it stays transparent to well-typed input (PY/GO counts unchanged, fuzz stays 450/450) and skips only the fragments a wrong TYPE makes meaningless; shape-defensive is_unverifiable and settlement rotation(); iterative why() with WHY_MAX_DEPTH. A blanket continue-on-schema-error was tried and rejected (it broke fuzz m_badhash by suppressing warnings a bad-VALUE record legitimately earns). Regression locked in tests/hostile.py (+18 checks). cmd@v1 re-runs conformance + hostile + fuzz + differential + ed25519; transcript pinned. Same verifier-totality class as the Gemini 3.1 Pro round, on the code path that pass had not reached.

subject.note: Kimi K3 full audit: fixed verify_store totality -- type-confused and hostile records no longer crash the whole-store run; settlement KeyError and deep-why RecursionError fixed. Battery green.
